---
title: "Journal"
author: "Guanyu"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: 
      latex_engine: xelatex
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r load-lib, include = FALSE}
library(readr)
library(sf) # for reading file
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr) # for percentile tranformation
library(e1071)   # for skewness() and kurtosis()
library(nortest)   # for Anderson–Darling test
library(gridExtra)   # for arranging multiple plots together
library(knitr)      # for kable() pretty tables
library(kableExtra) # for HTML/PDF styling (optional)
library(forcats)
library(ggrepel)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)
library(tidyverse)
library(reshape2)
library(viridis)
library(mclust)
library(clue)
source("Proprocessing.R")
```

# Exploaratory Data Analysis

## Loading data

```{r load-data, message = FALSE}
# read main data
simd <- read_csv("simd2020_withgeog/simd2020_withinds.csv")

# read shapefile to acquire geometries
simd_shp <- st_read("simd2020_withgeog/sc_dz_11.shp")

# merge the files using key "Data_Zone"
simd_joined <- simd_shp %>%
  left_join(simd, by = c("DataZone" = "Data_Zone"))
```

## Visualization over all domains' rank

```{r overall-ranking-plot, fig.width=14, fig.height=10, dpi=300, echo = FALSE}
# data frame that use to create domain-ranking plots
simd_domains <- simd_joined %>%
  select(DataZone,
         SIMD2020v2_Rank,
         SIMD2020v2_Income_Domain_Rank,
         SIMD2020_Employment_Domain_Rank,
         SIMD2020_Health_Domain_Rank,
         SIMD2020_Education_Domain_Rank,
         SIMD2020_Access_Domain_Rank,
         SIMD2020_Crime_Domain_Rank,
         SIMD2020_Housing_Domain_Rank,
         geometry) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("SIMD2020"),
    names_to = "Domain",
    values_to = "Rank"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Domain = case_when(
      grepl("Income", Domain) ~ "Income",
      grepl("Employment", Domain) ~ "Employment",
      grepl("Health", Domain) ~ "Health",
      grepl("Education", Domain) ~ "Education",
      grepl("Access", Domain) ~ "Access",
      grepl("Crime", Domain) ~ "Crime",
      grepl("Housing", Domain) ~ "Housing",
      grepl("Rank$", Domain) ~ "Overall",
      TRUE ~ Domain
    ),
    Domain = fct_relevel(Domain,
                         "Overall", "Income", "Employment",
                         "Health", "Education", "Access",
                         "Crime", "Housing")
  )

p_rank <- ggplot(simd_domains) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Rank), colour = NA) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", direction = -1,
                       name = "Deprivation Rank") +
  facet_wrap(~ Domain, ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "SIMD 2020 Domain Deprivation Ranks Across Scotland",
    subtitle = "1 = Most Deprived, 6,976 = Least Deprived",
    caption = "Source: Scottish Government (SIMD 2020v2)"
  )

ggsave(
  "figures/simd_domain_ranks.png",
  plot = p_rank,
  width = 11,
  height = 8.5,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300
)
```

## Descriptive statistics for all indicators

```{r all-indicators}

indicator_cols <- c(
  "income_rate", "income_count",
  "employment_rate", "employment_count",
  "CIF", "ALCOHOL", "DRUG", "SMR", "DEPRESS",
  "LBWT", "EMERG", "Attendance", "Attainment",
  "no_qualifications", "not_participating", "University",
  "crime_count", "crime_rate", "overcrowded_count",
  "nocentralheating_count", "overcrowded_rate",
  "nocentralheating_rate", "drive_petrol", "drive_GP",
  "drive_post", "drive_primary", "drive_retail",
  "drive_secondary", "PT_GP", "PT_post", "PT_retail", "broadband"
)

# Convert percentile to numeric value
simd_ind <- simd %>%
  select(any_of(indicator_cols)) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%"))))

```


```{r stattable-for-all, echo = FALSE}
stats_table <- lapply(simd_ind, describe_var) %>%
  bind_rows(.id = "Indicator") %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      c(mean, median, sd, skewness, kurtosis),
      ~round(.x, 2)
    ), # show statistics in 2 digits
    p_value = round(p_value, 4)   # show p-value to 4 digits
  )

stats_table <- stats_table %>%
  mutate(z_test = ifelse(p_value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", p_value)))

kable(stats_table, caption = "Descriptive Statistics and Normality Tests for SIMD Indicators")

library(kableExtra)
tab <- kbl(stats_table, caption = "Descriptive Statistics and Normality Tests for SIMD Indicators") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

save_kable(tab, "stats_table.png")
```

```{r density-plots-for-all, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12,, dpi=300, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
indicator_domains <- tribble(
  ~Indicator,                ~Domain,
  # Income
  "income_rate",             "Income",
  "income_count",            "Income",

  # Employment
  "employment_rate",         "Employment",
  "employment_count",        "Employment",

  # Health
  "CIF",                     "Health",
  "ALCOHOL",                 "Health",
  "DRUG",                    "Health",
  "SMR",                     "Health",
  "DEPRESS",                 "Health",
  "LBWT",                    "Health",
  "EMERG",                   "Health",

  # Education
  "Attendance",              "Education",
  "Attainment",              "Education",
  "no_qualifications",       "Education",
  "not_participating",       "Education",
  "University",              "Education",

  # Access
  "drive_petrol",            "Access",
  "drive_GP",                "Access",
  "drive_post",              "Access",
  "drive_primary",           "Access",
  "drive_retail",            "Access",
  "drive_secondary",         "Access",
  "PT_GP",                   "Access",
  "PT_post",                 "Access",
  "PT_retail",               "Access",
  "broadband",               "Access",

  # Crime
  "crime_count",             "Crime",
  "crime_rate",              "Crime",

  # Housing
  "overcrowded_count",       "Housing",
  "nocentralheating_count",  "Housing",
  "overcrowded_rate",        "Housing",
  "nocentralheating_rate",   "Housing"
)

simd_long <- simd_ind %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = where(is.numeric),
    names_to = "Indicator",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  left_join(indicator_domains, by = "Indicator") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Domain))

simd_long <- simd_long %>%
  mutate(
    # Ensure Domain appears in logical order
    Domain = fct_relevel(Domain,
                         "Income", "Employment", "Health",
                         "Education", "Access", "Crime", "Housing"),
    # Reorder Indicator factor sequentially by Domain grouping
    Indicator = factor(Indicator,
      levels = indicator_domains$Indicator
    )
  )

domain_colors <- c(
  "Income"      = "#1f77b4",  # blue
  "Employment"  = "#2ca02c",  # green
  "Health"      = "#d62728",  # red
  "Education"   = "#9467bd",  # purple
  "Access"      = "#ff7f0e",  # orange
  "Crime"       = "#17becf",  # teal
  "Housing"     = "#8c564b"   # brown
)

p_dist <- ggplot(simd_long, aes(x = Value, fill = Domain, color = Domain)) +
  geom_density(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ Indicator, scales = "free", ncol = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = domain_colors, name = "Domain") +
  scale_color_manual(values = domain_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
   strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
   axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
   axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
   axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
   legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
   legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
   panel.spacing = unit(0.8, "lines"),   # extra breathing space between panels
   plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
   plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5)
) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of SIMD 2020 Indicators",
    subtitle = "Indicators grouped sequentially by domain, with domain-specific colours",
    caption = "Source: Scottish Government (SIMD 2020v2)"
  )

ggsave(
  "figures/simd_indicator_distributions.png",
  plot = p_dist,
  width = 11,
  height = 8.5,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300
)

```

# Joint board area

```{r joint-board-map, echo = FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=12, dpi=300}
joint_board_lookup <- tribble(
  ~Council_area,                          ~Joint_Board_area,
  "East Ayrshire",                       "Ayrshire",
  "North Ayrshire",                      "Ayrshire",
  "South Ayrshire",                      "Ayrshire",
  "Scottish Borders",                    "Borders",
  "Clackmannanshire",                    "Central Scotland",
  "Falkirk",                             "Central Scotland",
  "Stirling",                            "Central Scotland",
  "Dumfries and Galloway",               "Dumfries and Galloway",
  "Argyll and Bute",                     "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "East Dunbartonshire",                 "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "West Dunbartonshire",                 "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "Fife",                                "Fife",
  "Aberdeen City",                       "Grampian",
  "Aberdeenshire",                       "Grampian",
  "Moray",                               "Grampian",
  "Glasgow City",                        "Glasgow",
  "Highland",                            "Highlands and Islands",
  "Na h-Eileanan an Iar",                "Highlands and Islands",
  "North Lanarkshire",                   "Lanarkshire",
  "South Lanarkshire",                   "Lanarkshire",
  "East Lothian",                        "Lothian",
  "City of Edinburgh",                   "Lothian",
  "Midlothian",                          "Lothian",
  "West Lothian",                        "Lothian",
  "Orkney Islands",                      "Orkney and Shetland",
  "Shetland Islands",                    "Orkney and Shetland",
  "East Renfrewshire",                   "Renfrewshire",
  "Inverclyde",                          "Renfrewshire",
  "Renfrewshire",                        "Renfrewshire",
  "Angus",                               "Tayside",
  "Dundee City",                         "Tayside",
  "Perth and Kinross",                   "Tayside"
)

# --- Step 1. Join lookup and count DataZones ---
simd_joined <- simd_joined %>%
  left_join(joint_board_lookup, by = c("Council_area" = "Council_area"))

joint_board_counts <- simd_joined %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(Joint_Board_area) %>%
  summarise(n_datazones = n())

# # --- Step 2. Build centroids safely (handle invalid geometry) ---
# joint_board_centroids <- simd_joined %>%
#   mutate(geometry = st_make_valid(geometry)) %>%
#   group_by(Joint_Board_area) %>%
#   summarise(geometry = st_union(geometry)) %>%
#   st_point_on_surface() %>%
#   left_join(joint_board_counts, by = "Joint_Board_area")
# 
# # --- Step 3. Reproject everything to EPSG:4326 ---
# simd_joined_4326 <- st_transform(simd_joined, 4326)
# joint_board_centroids_4326 <- st_transform(joint_board_centroids, 4326)
# 
# # --- Step 4. Create legend labels ---
# simd_joined_4326 <- simd_joined_4326 %>%
#   left_join(joint_board_counts, by = "Joint_Board_area") %>%
#   mutate(legend_label = paste0(Joint_Board_area, " (", comma(n_datazones), ")"))
# 
# centroid_coords <- st_coordinates(joint_board_centroids_4326) %>%
#   as.data.frame() %>%
#   bind_cols(st_drop_geometry(joint_board_centroids_4326))
# 
# # --- Step 6. Plot the map ---
# p_jba <- ggplot(simd_joined_4326) +
#   geom_sf(aes(fill = legend_label), color = NA) +
# 
#   geom_text_repel(
#     data = centroid_coords,
#     aes(X, Y, label = paste0(Joint_Board_area, " (", n_datazones, ")")),
#     fontface = "bold",
#     color = "black",
#     size = 3.8,
#     box.padding = 0.7,
#     point.padding = 0.5,
#     segment.color = "grey40",
#     segment.size = 0.3,
#     max.overlaps = Inf,
#     show.legend = FALSE
#   ) +
# 
#   # Legend now only shows area names
#   scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", name = "Joint Board Area") +
# 
#   guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 1)) +  # neatly wrap legend
#   coord_sf() +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Scottish Joint Board Areas",
#   ) +
#   theme(
#     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15, hjust = 0.5),
#     plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
#     legend.position = "right",
#     legend.box = "vertical",
#     legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
#     legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
#     plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
#   )
# 
# ggsave(
#   "figures/joint_board_area.png",
#   plot = p_jba,
#   width = 11,
#   height = 8.5,
#   units = "in",
#   dpi = 300
# )
# p_jba
```
# 
# # Lothian and Glasgow
# 
# ## Data Description
# 
# ```{r Lothian-stattable, echo = FALSE}
# simd_ind <- simd_joined %>%
#   filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
#   st_drop_geometry() %>%
#   select(any_of(indicator_cols)) %>%
#   mutate(across(where(is.character), ~as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%"))))
# 
# stats_table <- lapply(simd_ind, describe_var) %>%
#   bind_rows(.id = "Indicator") %>%
#   mutate(
#     across(
#       c(mean, median, sd, skewness, kurtosis),
#       ~round(.x, 2)
#     ), # show statistics in 2 digits
#     p_value = round(p_value, 4)   # show p-value to 4 digits
#   )
# 
# stats_table <- stats_table %>%
#   mutate(p_value = ifelse(p_value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", p_value)))
# 
# kable(stats_table, caption = "Descriptive Statistics for Lothian's Indicators")
# ```
# 
# ```{r Lanarkshire-stattable, echo = FALSE}
# simd_ind <- simd_joined %>%
#   filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lanarkshire") %>%
#   st_drop_geometry() %>%
#   select(any_of(indicator_cols)) %>%
#   mutate(across(where(is.character), ~as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%"))))
# 
# stats_table <- lapply(simd_ind, describe_var) %>%
#   bind_rows(.id = "Indicator") %>%
#   mutate(
#     across(
#       c(mean, median, sd, skewness, kurtosis),
#       ~round(.x, 2)
#     ), # show statistics in 2 digits
#     p_value = round(p_value, 4)   # show p-value to 4 digits
#   )
# 
# stats_table <- stats_table %>%
#   mutate(p_value = ifelse(p_value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", p_value)))
# 
# kable(stats_table, caption = "Descriptive Statistics for Lanarkshire's Indicators")
# ```
# 
# ```{r Glasgow-stattable, echo = FALSE}
# simd_ind <- simd_joined %>%
#   filter(Joint_Board_area == "Glasgow") %>%
#   st_drop_geometry() %>%
#   select(any_of(indicator_cols)) %>%
#   mutate(across(where(is.character), ~as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%"))))
# 
# stats_table <- lapply(simd_ind, describe_var) %>%
#   bind_rows(.id = "Indicator") %>%
#   mutate(
#     across(
#       c(mean, median, sd, skewness, kurtosis),
#       ~round(.x, 2)
#     ), # show statistics in 2 digits
#     p_value = round(p_value, 4)   # show p-value to 4 digits
#   )
# 
# stats_table <- stats_table %>%
#   mutate(p_value = ifelse(p_value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", p_value)))
# 
# kable(stats_table, caption = "Descriptive Statistics for Glasgow's Indicators")
# ```
# 
# ```{r density-plot-lothian-glasgow, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12,, dpi=300, echo = FALSE}
# library(dplyr)
# library(tidyr)
# library(ggplot2)
# library(forcats)
# library(stringr)
# 
# boards_to_compare <- c("Lothian", "Glasgow","Lanarkshire")
# 
# simd_clean <- simd_joined %>%
#   st_drop_geometry() %>%
#   mutate(across(any_of(indicator_domains$Indicator),
#                 ~ as.numeric(str_remove(as.character(.x), "%"))))
# 
# #  Filter for selected Joint Board Areas and reshape data
# simd_compare <- simd_clean %>%
#   filter(Joint_Board_area %in% boards_to_compare) %>%
#   select(Joint_Board_area, any_of(indicator_domains$Indicator)) %>%
#   pivot_longer(
#     cols = -Joint_Board_area,
#     names_to = "Indicator",
#     values_to = "Value"
#   ) %>%
#   left_join(indicator_domains, by = "Indicator") %>%
#   filter(!is.na(Domain)) %>%
#   mutate(
#     Joint_Board_area = factor(Joint_Board_area, levels = boards_to_compare),
#     Domain = fct_relevel(Domain, "Income","Employment","Health",
#                          "Education","Access","Crime","Housing"),
#     Indicator = factor(Indicator, levels = indicator_domains$Indicator)
#   )
# 
# # Define colors for the two areas
# board_colors <- c("Lothian" = "#9467bd", "Glasgow" = "#2ca02c", "Lanarkshire" = "yellow")
# 
# # Plot densities side-by-side
# ggplot(simd_compare, aes(x = Value, fill = Joint_Board_area)) +
#   geom_density(alpha = 0.5, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
#   facet_wrap(~ Indicator, scales = "free", ncol = 4) +
#   scale_fill_manual(values = board_colors, name = "Joint Board Area") +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
#   theme(
#     strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
#     axis.title = element_blank(),
#     axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
#     axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
#     legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
#     legend.text  = element_text(size = 10),
#     legend.position = "bottom",
#     panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
#     plot.title = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
#     plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
#     plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 1)
#   ) +
#   labs(
#     title = "Comparison of Indicator Distributions between Lothian, Glasgow and Lanarkshire",
#     subtitle = "Density plots of SIMD 2020v2 indicators for selected Joint Board Areas",
#     caption = "Source: Scottish Government (SIMD 2020v2)"
#   )
# 
# ```
# 
# ```{r deprivation-plot-lothian-glasgow, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12,, dpi=300, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# library(ggplot2)
# library(dplyr)
# library(sf)
# library(viridis)
# library(patchwork)
# 
# # --- 1. Count DataZones per Joint Board Area ---
# area_counts <- simd_joined %>%
#   st_drop_geometry() %>%
#   group_by(Joint_Board_area) %>%
#   summarise(n_datazones = n())
# 
# # --- 2. Select target areas ---
# areas_of_interest <- c("Lothian", "Glasgow", "Lanarkshire")
# 
# simd_subset <- simd_joined %>%
#   filter(Joint_Board_area %in% areas_of_interest) %>%
#   left_join(area_counts, by = "Joint_Board_area")
# 
# # --- 3. Compute within-board deprivation ranks ---
# simd_subset <- simd_subset %>%
#   group_by(Joint_Board_area) %>%
#   mutate(
#     Local_Rank = rank(SIMD2020v2_Rank, ties.method = "first"),
#     Local_Rank_Percentile = percent_rank(SIMD2020v2_Rank) * 100
#   ) %>%
#   ungroup()
# 
# # --- 4. Create Joint Board boundaries for combined map ---
# joint_board_boundaries <- simd_subset %>%
#   st_make_valid() %>%
#   group_by(Joint_Board_area) %>%
#   summarise(geometry = st_union(geometry)) %>%
#   ungroup()
# 
# # --- 5. Assign distinct colours for borders ---
# border_colors <- c(
#   "Glasgow"      = "#1b9e77",
#   "Lanarkshire"  = "#d95f02",
#   "Lothian"      = "#7570b3"
# )
# 
# # --- 6. Combined map with distinctive borders ---
# p_combined <- ggplot(simd_subset) +
#   geom_sf(aes(fill = SIMD2020v2_Rank), colour = NA) +
#   geom_sf(data = joint_board_boundaries,
#           aes(color = Joint_Board_area),
#           fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8, show.legend = TRUE) +
#   scale_fill_viridis_c(
#     option = "plasma",
#     direction = -1,
#     name = "Deprivation Rank\n(National)",
#     na.value = "lightgrey"
#   ) +
#   scale_color_manual(
#     values = border_colors,
#     name = "Joint Board Area",
#     guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = NA, linewidth = 1.5))
#   ) +
#   labs(
#     title = "Combined View with Distinct Joint Board Boundaries",
#     caption = "Source: Scottish Government (SIMD 2020v2)"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
#   theme(
#     legend.position = "right",
#     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
#     plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
#     axis.text = element_blank(),
#     axis.ticks = element_blank(),
#     panel.grid = element_blank()
#   )
# 
# # --- 7. Separate within-area maps (local scales use number of DataZones) ---
# p_list <- lapply(areas_of_interest, function(area) {
#   df <- filter(simd_subset, Joint_Board_area == area)
# 
#   ggplot(df) +
#     geom_sf(aes(fill = Local_Rank), color = NA) +
#     scale_fill_viridis_c(
#       option = "plasma",
#       direction = -1,
#       limits = c(1, max(df$Local_Rank, na.rm = TRUE)),
#       name = paste0("Deprivation Rank\n(1–", max(df$Local_Rank, na.rm = TRUE), ")"),
#       na.value = "lightgrey"
#     ) +
#     labs(
#       title = area,
#       subtitle = paste0("(", unique(df$n_datazones), " DataZones)")
#     ) +
#     theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
#     theme(
#       legend.position = "bottom",
#       legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
#       legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
#       axis.text = element_blank(),
#       axis.ticks = element_blank(),
#       panel.grid = element_blank(),
#       plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
#       plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5)
#     )
# })
# 
# # --- 8. Arrange the smaller maps neatly ---
# ## p_list      <- list of the three separate maps (Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Lothian)
# p_facets <- wrap_plots(p_list, ncol = 3)
# 
# # A real separator row (blank plot with title/subtitle)
# p_sep <- ggplot() +
#   theme_void() +
#   labs(title = "Separate View of Each Area",
#        subtitle = "Within-Area Deprivation Rankings (Independent Colour Scales)") +
#   theme(
#     plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 2)),
#     plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(t = 0))
#   )
# 
# # Stack: combined map / separator / small maps
# final_plot <-
#   (p_combined / p_sep / p_facets) +
#   plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.10, 1.3)) &                       # adjust the middle height to taste
#   plot_annotation(
#     title = "Deprivation Across Lothian, Glasgow, and Lanarkshire (SIMD 2020v2)",
#     theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5))
#   )
# 
# final_plot
# 
# ```
# 
# ```{r correlation-map, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12,, dpi=300, echo = FALSE}
# library(tidyverse)
# library(reshape2)
# library(patchwork)
# library(viridis)
# 
# # --- 0. Define indicator order by domain for readability ---
indicator_order <- indicator_domains$Indicator
domain_colors <- c(
  "Income" = "#1f77b4",
  "Employment" = "#2ca02c",
  "Health" = "#d62728",
  "Education" = "#9467bd",
  "Access" = "#ff7f0e",
  "Crime" = "#17becf",
  "Housing" = "#8c564b"
)


# --- 1. Apply function for both areas ---
p_lothian <- plot_corr_heatmap(simd_joined, "Lothian")
p_glasgow <- plot_corr_heatmap(simd_joined, "Glasgow")
P <- plot_corr_heatmap(simd_joined)

# --- 2. Combine side by side with patchwork ---
(p_lothian / p_glasgow / P) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Comparison of Correlation Structures between Lothian and Glasgow",
    subtitle = "Indicators grouped by domain; values represent Pearson correlation coefficients",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5)
    )
  )

```

# Lothian's Clustering

## Preprocessing

```{r Lothian-Clustering-preprocessing}
simd_lothian <- simd_joined %>%
  filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(DataZone, all_of(indicator_cols)) %>%
  # convert only character indicators, never DataZone
  mutate(across(where(is.character) & !matches("^DataZone$"),
                ~ as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%"))))

# display missing values

na_summary <- simd_lothian %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(intersect(indicator_cols, names(simd_lothian))),
                   list(Missing_Count = ~ sum(is.na(.)),
                        Percent_Missing = ~ mean(is.na(.)) * 100),
                   .names = "{.col}.{.fn}")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to = c("Indicator", ".value"),
    names_sep = "\\."
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Missing_Count))

kable(na_summary %>% filter(Missing_Count > 0),
      digits = 2,
      caption = "Indicators with Missing Values in Lothian")

lothian_ind <- indicator_cols[indicator_cols != "Attendance"]

simd_lothian <- simd_joined %>%
  filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(DataZone, all_of(lothian_ind)) %>%
  # convert only character indicators, never DataZone
  mutate(across(where(is.character) & !matches("^DataZone$"),
                ~ as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%")))) %>%
  # drop rows missing in any indicator (keep DataZone untouched)
  tidyr::drop_na(all_of(lothian_ind))

lothian_data <- simd_lothian %>%
    arrange(DataZone)

lothian_data_scaled <- lothian_data %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(lothian_ind), ~ scale(.)[,1]))

```

```{r lothian-pca, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12, dpi=300}
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)

domain_vars <- indicator_domains %>%
  filter(Indicator %in% colnames(lothian_data)) %>%
  group_by(Domain) %>%
  summarise(vars = list(Indicator)) %>%
  deframe()

# Run PCA for each domain and store the PCA object
pca_results <- lapply(names(domain_vars), function(domain) {
  vars <- domain_vars[[domain]]
  
  # Keep only variables present in the dataset
  valid_vars <- intersect(vars, colnames(lothian_data))
  
  if (length(valid_vars) == 0) {
    message(paste("Skipping domain:", domain, "(no valid variables)"))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # --- 1. Run PCA ---
  pca <- prcomp(lothian_data[, valid_vars, drop = FALSE], scale. = TRUE)
  
  # --- 2. Compute variance explained ---
  var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)
  var_df <- tibble(
    PC = factor(paste0("PC", 1:length(var_explained)), 
                levels = paste0("PC", 1:length(var_explained))),
    Variance = var_explained
  )
  
    # --- 3. Create visualization ---
  plot <- ggplot(var_df, aes(x = PC, y = Variance)) +
    geom_col(fill = "#4B9CD3") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Variance * 100)), 
              vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
    labs(
      title = paste(domain),
      x = "Principal Component",
      y = "Proportion of Variance Explained"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )
  
 # Return PCA object and its plot
  return(list(pca = pca, plot = plot))
})

# Assign domain names to list elements
names(pca_results) <- names(domain_vars)
```

```{r lothian-pc-visuals, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12, dpi=30}
# Combine all PCA variance plots into a grid

pca_plots <- lapply(pca_results, function(x) x$plot)
pca_grid <- wrap_plots(pca_plots) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Domain-wise PCA Results for Lothian Indicators",
    subtitle = "Each plot shows variance explained by principal components within its domain",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
  )
pca_grid

```

```{r lothian-pca-extraction}
library(purrr)
library(tibble)

pca_domains <- c("Health", "Education", "Access")

# --- 1. Extract domain-wise PCs explaining 80% cumulative variance ---
domain_pc_list <- purrr::map(names(pca_results), function(domain) {
  
  # Skip domains not in target list
  if (!(domain %in% pca_domains)) return(NULL)
  
  res <- pca_results[[domain]]
  
  # Safety checks
  if (is.null(res) || !"pca" %in% names(res)) return(NULL)
  pca <- res$pca
  if (is.null(pca$x)) return(NULL)
  
  # Compute variance explained
  var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)
  cum_var <- cumsum(var_explained)
  
  # Retain PCs until cumulative variance >= 80%
  keep_pcs <- which(cum_var >= 0.80)[1]   # first PC index that reaches 80%
  keep_pcs <- 1:keep_pcs                  # retain all previous ones
  
  # Extract retained PC scores
  pc_data <- as.data.frame(pca$x[, keep_pcs, drop = FALSE])
  colnames(pc_data) <- paste0(domain, "_PC", keep_pcs)
  
  tibble(
    DataZone = simd_lothian$DataZone[seq_len(nrow(pc_data))],
    pc_data
  )
})


# --- 2. Combine domain PC datasets ---
lothian_pc <- domain_pc_list %>%
  compact() %>%
  reduce(full_join, by = "DataZone") %>%
  drop_na()

simd_lothian <- simd_lothian %>%
  left_join(lothian_pc)

lothian_scaled <- lothian_data %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(lothian_ind), scale))
```

```{r Lothian-Scaled-table}
stats_table <- lapply(lothian_data %>% select(-DataZone), describe_var) %>%
  bind_rows(.id = "Indicator") %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      c(mean, median, sd, skewness, kurtosis),
      ~round(.x, 2)
    ), # show statistics in 2 digits
    p_value = round(p_value, 4)   # show p-value to 4 digits
  )
stats_table <- stats_table %>%
  mutate(p_value = ifelse(p_value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", p_value)))

kable(stats_table, caption = "Descriptive Statistics and Normality Tests for scaled Lothian's indicators")
```

```{r Lothian-scaled-visual, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12, dpi=300}

# --- Identify PCA columns automatically ---
pca_cols <- names(lothian_data)[str_detect(names(lothian_data), "_PC")]

# Create a tibble mapping PCA column names to their domains
pca_indicators <- tibble(
  Indicator = pca_cols,
  Domain = str_extract(pca_cols, "^[A-Za-z]+")  # take the prefix before "_PC"
)

# Combine raw indicator-domain mapping with PCA mapping
indicator_domains_expanded <- bind_rows(indicator_domains, pca_indicators)

# Reorder indicators within each domain ---
indicator_domains_expanded <- indicator_domains_expanded %>%
  group_by(Domain) %>%
  arrange(Indicator, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  ungroup()

# Ensure domains appear in logical order
indicator_domains_expanded <- indicator_domains_expanded %>%
  mutate(
    Domain = fct_relevel(Domain,
                         "Income", "Employment", "Health",
                         "Education", "Access", "Crime", "Housing")
  )

simd_long <- lothian_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = where(is.numeric),
    names_to = "Indicator",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  left_join(indicator_domains_expanded, by = "Indicator") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Domain)) %>%
  mutate(
    Indicator = factor(
      Indicator,
      levels = indicator_domains_expanded$Indicator
    )
  )

domain_colors <- c(
  "Income"      = "#1f77b4",
  "Employment"  = "#2ca02c",
  "Health"      = "#d62728",
  "Education"   = "#9467bd",
  "Access"      = "#ff7f0e",
  "Crime"       = "#17becf",
  "Housing"     = "#8c564b"
)

ggplot(simd_long, aes(x = Value, fill = Domain, color = Domain)) +
  geom_density(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~ Indicator, scales = "free", ncol = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = domain_colors, name = "Domain") +
  scale_color_manual(values = domain_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Scaled Lothian's Indicators (Including Domain PCs)",
    subtitle = "Each facet shows indicator or PCA distribution by domain",
    caption = "Source: Scottish Government (SIMD 2020v2)"
  )

```

```{r lothian-Correlation-map,fig.width= 10, fig.height=12, dpi=300}
# --- 1. Select numeric columns for correlation ---
lothian_numeric <- lothian_data %>% 
  select(where(is.numeric))

# --- 2. Compute correlation matrix ---
corr_matrix <- cor(lothian_numeric, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# --- 3. Convert to long format for ggplot ---
corr_long <- melt(corr_matrix, varnames = c("Var1", "Var2"), value.name = "Correlation")

# --- 4. Create combined indicator-domain mapping (including PCs) ---
pca_cols <- names(lothian_data)[grepl("_PC", names(lothian_data))]
pca_indicators <- tibble(
  Indicator = pca_cols,
  Domain = stringr::str_extract(pca_cols, "^[A-Za-z]+")
)

indicator_domains_all <- bind_rows(indicator_domains, pca_indicators) %>%
  mutate(
    Domain = fct_relevel(Domain,
                         "Income", "Employment", "Health", 
                         "Education", "Access", "Crime", "Housing")
  )

# --- 5. Apply indicator ordering within domains ---
ordered_indicators <- indicator_domains_all %>%
  arrange(Domain, Indicator) %>%
  pull(Indicator)

ordered_indicators <- intersect(ordered_indicators, colnames(corr_matrix))

corr_long <- corr_long %>%
  mutate(
    Var1 = factor(Var1, levels = ordered_indicators),
    Var2 = factor(Var2, levels = ordered_indicators)
  )

# --- 6. Plot correlation heatmap ---
ggplot(corr_long, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#2166ac", mid = "white", high = "#b2182b", midpoint = 0,
    name = "Correlation", limits = c(-1, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation Heatmap of All Indicators (Including PCA Components)",
    x = "Indicator",
    y = "Indicator",
    caption = "Source: Scottish Government (SIMD 2020v2)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r lothian-pc-correlation, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12, dpi=300}
pc_data <- lothian_data %>%
  select(all_of(pca_cols), "income_rate", "employment_rate", "crime_rate", "nocentralheating_rate", "overcrowded_rate"  )

corr_matrix <- cor(pc_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# --- 3. Convert to long format for ggplot ---
corr_long <- melt(corr_matrix, varnames = c("Var1", "Var2"), value.name = "Correlation")

# --- 6. Plot correlation heatmap ---
ggplot(corr_long, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#2166ac", mid = "white", high = "#b2182b", midpoint = 0,
    name = "Correlation", limits = c(-1, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation Heatmap of Lothian's Main Principal Components ",
    x = "Indicator",
    y = "Indicator",
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```


```{r lothian-raw-correlation, fig.width= 10, fig.height=12, dpi=300}
raw_ind <- indicator_domains_expanded %>% 
  filter (Domain %in% c("Health", "Education", "Access")) %>%
  filter(!Indicator %in% c(pca_cols,"Attendance")) %>%
  pull(Indicator)

raw_data <- lothian_data %>%
  select(all_of(raw_ind))

corr_matrix <- cor(raw_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# --- 3. Convert to long format for ggplot ---
corr_long <- melt(corr_matrix, varnames = c("Var1", "Var2"), value.name = "Correlation")

# --- 6. Plot correlation heatmap ---
ggplot(corr_long, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#2166ac", mid = "white", high = "#b2182b", midpoint = 0,
    name = "Correlation", limits = c(-1, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation Heatmap of Lothian's Raw Indicators ",
    x = "Indicator",
    y = "Indicator",
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.key.height = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```
